trade.summary: Saudi Arabia is South Korea's largest source of crude oil, accounting for approximately 30% of Korea's oil imports. Korea exports petrochemicals, automobiles (Hyundai-Kia), electronics, and construction services to Saudi Arabia. Korean construction conglomerates have built major infrastructure across the Kingdom. The relationship is strongly asymmetric, shaped by Korea's energy import dependence.

diplomatic.summary: Korea-Saudi relations are primarily energy-driven but have expanded into Vision 2030 collaboration. Saudi Arabia sees Korea as a technology and construction partner for its diversification goals. Korea has participated in NEOM and other megaproject construction. Both sides maintain respectful relations and there is growing people-to-people connection through Korean culture (K-pop, K-drama have significant Saudi followings).

military.summary: Korea has expanded defense exports globally and Saudi Arabia is a potential market. Korean defense systems (K9 howitzers, coastal defense) have been marketed in Saudi Arabia. No formal defense treaty. Korea's focus remains on the Korean Peninsula deterrence mission. Saudi Arabia purchases primarily US, European, and UK weapons.

financial.summary: Saudi Aramco has a strategic stake in S-OIL, one of Korea's largest refiners, ensuring downstream processing of Saudi crude in Korea. Saudi PIF has made investments in Korean companies and funds. Korean construction conglomerates (Samsung C&T, Hyundai E&C, SK) have generated enormous revenues from Saudi projects, with new Vision 2030 projects continuing to flow.

energy.summary: Saudi Arabia is South Korea's number one crude oil supplier, a relationship of paramount strategic importance. Saudi Aramco's ownership stake in S-OIL refinery in Korea deepens this relationship. Both countries are exploring hydrogen cooperation as Saudi Arabia positions for green hydrogen exports. The energy relationship is the anchor of the bilateral.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation driven by Vision 2030. Korea-Saudi research ties are developing in renewable energy, desalination, and digital technology. KAUST-Korean university partnerships are growing. Korean companies bring technology transfer as part of Vision 2030 localization requirements.
